pages.text:
This book is dedicated to my parents, John and Rosa, who made me all that I am;  
my brothers and siblings-in-law, John, Doug, Annemarie, Melissa, Pam, and  
Charlie, who keep me grounded; my children, Brie, Belle, Spencer, and  
Kayla, who remind me to look to the horizon; and to my wife,  
Hilary, whose love and support make it all worthwhile.
A special thanks to Randy Braddom, MD, for the legacy that started  
this textbook and for his ongoing support.
Much appreciation to my amazingly supportive colleagues, staff, and trainees all across 
Virginia Commonwealth University—including in the School of Medicine and the 
Department of Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation—for their mentorship,  
their friendship, and the time to complete this project.
Thank you to the wonderful members of the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs  
for all they do for America’s veterans and service members and  
for allowing me the time to complete this project.
Thank you to the tens of thousands of patients and their family members who allowed  
me to participate in their care over the years; you have taught me so much.
In sum, a heartfelt thanks to my family, friends, and other loved ones for your  
ongoing support that allows me to pursue my work and career.
Descargado para juan camilo sarmiento (z0500754a@saludcastillayleon.es) en Government of Castile and Leon Ministry of Health de ClinicalKey.es por Elsevier en 
junio 02, 2026. Para uso personal exclusivamente. No se permiten otros usos sin autorización. Copyright ©2026. Elsevier Inc. Todos los derechos reservados.
